title: blazewrit harness — Triage step structured_output (M1 = SHAPE)
description: structured_output schema for the TRIAGE step's OUTPUT, enforced at token-generation (grammar-constrained generation; §16). Triage is a STATELESS classification function: (input)->(output), one invocation = one output, no persistence inside the step (the OUTPUT still persists to Postgres step_runs jsonb per §5, but Triage itself reads/writes no disk and holds no flow state). The output is a 4-TYPE EXHAUSTIVE DISCRIMINATED UNION keyed on `result`: proceed | none | ambiguous | error. Grammar forces exactly one branch with that branch's required fields, so the model cannot emit a proceed without a flow_type, an error without a reason enum, etc. There are NO measured counts in this step — complexity_signal is an ENUM (high|medium|low|none), deliberately NOT a CountClaim (Triage does no code analysis, runs no tools, so it has no raw_stdout to hash). There are NO tool-dependent fields, so NO Measured|Omitted degrade union appears here (Triage invokes no degradable tool). matched_rows are signal-table SIGNAL-ROW ids — §5 RowRefs (Postgres row ids), never file paths. M2 validator contracts (TRUTH, not grammar) are listed in x-validator-contract annotations and in the step manifest; grammar gives shape only.

result.description: DISCRIMINANT for the 4-type exhaustive output union. Exactly one branch is selected by this enum; the matching oneOf branch fixes that branch's required fields.

oneOf.$comment: BRANCH 1 — proceed: a single flow_type was classified (signal-table single row match, or strict superset, P0-over-BugFix, or verb/target>=2 => compound).
oneOf.title: proceed

classification_metadata.description: How the classification was reached. matched_rows are signal-table row refs; confidence + complexity_signal are enums (NOT counts).

matched_rows.description: The signal-table SIGNAL-ROW id(s) this classification matched. These are signal-table LITERAL ids from the Triage Signal Table — append-only string ids naming WHICH signal row matched (e.g. 'S1','S15'), NOT Postgres step_runs RowRefs and NEVER file paths. A proceed cites ONLY CITABLE component rows S1..S15 — BOTH S0 ('No actionable signal', maps to result=none) AND S16 ('Multiple blockers...', the compound META-row) are documented-but-never-emitted rows FORBIDDEN here, so the proceed item pattern excludes both even though the shared SignalRowId def admits them for documentation of the full table. S16 is the meta-row that flow_type=compound maps to, but compound.matched_rows cites the >=2 COMPONENT rows S1..S15 that drove it, NOT S16 itself (S16 is never a matched-row token, exactly like S0). A single-row match (or strict superset) yields confidence=high; compound (verb/target>=2) lists the 2+ component rows. Empty is not allowed for a proceed — a proceed must cite at least one matched signal row (the basis for the flow_type).

items.$comment: proceed-branch matched_rows are CITABLE component rows ONLY (S1..S15). S0 and S16 are both excluded by this narrower pattern: SignalRowId admits S0 (=>result=none) and S16 (compound meta-row) for full-table documentation, but neither is ever the cited basis of a proceed — compound cites its >=2 component rows S1..S15, not the S16 meta-row.

confidence.$comment: Shared Confidence enum (high|medium|low) — all three are reachable, legitimately-produced proceed values. high = (FIRST classification ONLY: reclassify_count==0 AND prior_evidence absent) exactly 1 signal row matched (or strict superset, in which case matched_rows cites ONLY the winning superset row — exactly one element, e.g. [S3] not [S2,S3]), clarifications unnecessary; OR flow_type=compound citing >=2 non-superset COMPONENT rows S1..S15 (NOT the S16 meta-row, which is never emitted in matched_rows — compound's defining cardinality of >=2 component rows IS its high-confidence basis — see confidence_consistent_with_match_cardinality, which exempts compound). high↔medium PRECEDENCE (reclassify re-confirm): the single-row high trigger is RESERVED for the first classification (reclassify_count==0, prior_evidence absent); ANY reclassify (reclassify_count>=1, prior_evidence present) re-confirming the SAME prior_flow_type with a single row is medium (stable re-confirmation), NEVER high — even when the current input alone strongly clears the dominant-row threshold. prior_evidence presence deterministically separates the high single-row case from the medium reclassify-re-confirm case, so they never co-match. compound is the SOLE exception: a compound classification is ALWAYS high regardless of reclassify/correction (compound rule dominates — see compound_confidence_high_dominates_reclassify). medium = matched but input mildly ambiguous: (clarifications used to confirm a single row, restricted to a NON-CORRECTING confirmation that keeps prior_flow_type — a clarifications-confirmed row that CORRECTS to a different flow is low(b), not medium), OR a reclassify where the CURRENT INPUT ITSELF independently re-supports prior_flow_type (current signal alone clears the prior row's dominant-row threshold), regardless of whether clarifications were used. low = dominant-but-weak SINGLE-ROW proceed: a single dominant row classifies, but the match is weak — either (a) prior_evidence supports the SAME flow_type yet the current input does NOT independently re-support that prior row (current signal alone is below the dominant-row threshold, marginal; prior_evidence carries the supporting weight), OR (b) a reclassify (reclassify_count>=1) narrowly corrects the prior classification to a DIFFERENT single flow (one component row). low(b) is SINGLE-ROW correction ONLY — a reclassify that newly classifies as COMPOUND (>=2 component rows, a different flow set) is high, not low(b) (compound rule dominates the correction rule — see compound_confidence_high_dominates_reclassify). medium vs low(a) PRECEDENCE (removes the same-flow re-confirm-under-reclassify overlap): the two are mutually exclusive on a single test — whether the CURRENT INPUT ALONE clears the prior row's dominant-row threshold: if yes => medium (current input itself re-confirms), if no (support depends on prior_evidence) => low(a). medium vs low(b) PRECEDENCE (removes the clarifications-confirm vs reclassify-correct overlap): the two are mutually exclusive on whether the current classification KEEPS prior_flow_type (same flow) or CORRECTS it (different flow) — a correcting reclassify to a different flow is ALWAYS low(b) regardless of whether clarifications were used; medium-via-clarifications is reserved for NON-correcting same-flow confirmations only. compound vs low(b) PRECEDENCE (compound-correction): a correcting reclassify whose result is compound (>=2 component rows) is high, not low(b); low(b) applies only when the correction is to a single dominant row. A single confidence value is thus determined for every (prior, current) pair — same-flow, single-row correcting, or compound. low signals downstream (esp. Investigate) that re-examination room remains; it is NOT the no-dominant-row case (that stays ambiguous).

complexity_signal.$comment: Shared ComplexitySignal enum (high|medium|low|none). Inferred task complexity feeding Step Depth Policy's deepen trigger. ENUM by design — NOT a CountClaim: Triage runs no tool and reads no code, so there is no raw_stdout to hash; this is a judgement signal, not a measured count.

oneOf.$comment: BRANCH 2 — none: input is not actionable (no concrete target / discussion / casual). Host LLM continues free conversation; A2A returns INTENT_NOT_ACTIONABLE.
oneOf.title: none

reasoning.description: Why the input is not actionable (no verb/target extracted, open brainstorming, casual exchange). Free text — channel handlers (user_session free-talk vs A2A INTENT_NOT_ACTIONABLE vs CI trigger-config error) consume it.

oneOf.$comment: BRANCH 3 — ambiguous: multiple signal rows matched with no superset (disposition=clarify), OR reclassify_count>=3 forced terminal escalation (disposition=escalate). The REQUIRED `disposition` enum is THE real discriminant closing H1 — terminal escalation is now an enum FIELD the orchestrator routes on (clarify -> re-invoke loop; escalate -> terminal halt), not a magic question string-match. Carries exactly ONE routing-blocking question (clarify) or one-line escalate reason (escalate).
oneOf.title: ambiguous

disposition.$comment: Shared Disposition enum (clarify|escalate). clarify = multiple non-superset rows; orchestrator surfaces `question` and re-invokes (loop). escalate = terminal: reclassify cap reached (reclassify_count>=3) or otherwise undecidable/upstream-deficient; orchestrator HALTS the flow and escalates, no re-invoke (loop prevention). This is the real enum discriminant H1/principle-3 depends on.

question.description: ONE routing-blocking clarification question when disposition=clarify (priority: verb > target > urgency > concern_count), OR a one-line escalation reason when disposition=escalate. For disposition=escalate there are two MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE escalate kinds, partitioned by reclassify_count: (1) the reclassify cap (reclassify_count>=3) forces question == the reserved cap string 'reclassify cap reached, manual intervention required' (reclassify_cap_forces_ambiguous_escalate, scoped to count>=3 only). The cap check STRICTLY PRECEDES classification, so when count>=3 NO classification runs — a two-flow tie can never be reached at cap. (2) any non-cap escalate (reclassify_count < 3: a two-flow tie that clarify cannot resolve, or otherwise undecidable / upstream-deficient) carries a free one-line reason naming the cause, which MUST NOT be the reserved cap string (noncap_escalate_question_is_nonempty_noncap_reason). The cap-forced escalation is an INPUT-driven rule the grammar cannot see (it depends on the injected reclassify_count); both are M2 validator_contracts gating question, not schema keywords.

oneOf.$comment: BRANCH 4 — error: GIBBERISH (parse failure / meaningless input) or INTERNAL (Triage self-failure). reason is a fixed enum.
oneOf.title: error

reason.description: Error cause enum. GIBBERISH = primary_input could not be parsed / is meaningless (Comprehend failed). INTERNAL = Triage itself failed.

FlowType.$comment: The 16 flow types from the Triage Signal Table. The canonical machine source is the flow-file STEMS at legacy/.blazewrit/flows/<id>.md, which use HYPHENS (bugfix-p0, bugfix-unreproducible, plan-standalone; all others single-word). This enum is the generated projection of those stems; README/DECISIONS §10 display names are human-facing. P0 and Unreproducible are DISTINCT Bug Fix variants (separate signal-table rows / separate flow files), not the same flow. 'none'/free-conversation is NOT a flow_type — it is the separate `result:none` branch, so it is intentionally absent from this enum.
FlowType.description: Triage flow_type — one of the 16 first-class flows the Signal Table maps to. Compound = verb/target>=2 (multiple blockers / multi-phase). Used only inside the proceed branch.

SignalRowId.$comment: Signal-table literal row id (Triage Signal Table). S1..S15 are the CITABLE component rows; S16 ('Multiple blockers...') is the compound META-row (maps documentation-wise to flow_type=compound) and S0 ('No actionable signal') is the non-flow row that maps to result=none. These are append-only signal-table ids, NOT Postgres step_runs RowRefs and NOT file paths. SCOPE NOTE: this def documents the FULL table (S0..S16) but is not directly used as an output carrier — proceed.matched_rows uses a narrower S1..S15 pattern (BOTH S0 and S16 forbidden: S0 has no flow_type, and compound cites its >=2 component rows S1..S15 rather than the S16 meta-row), and the none branch carries no matched_rows (S0 needs no carrier; reasoning suffices). Thus S0 and S16 are documented table rows, never emitted output tokens — exactly parallel meta-rows.
SignalRowId.description: A Triage Signal Table row id, S0..S16 (full table). NOTE: the proceed branch's matched_rows narrows to S1..S15; S0 (=>result=none) and S16 (compound meta-row, never cited — compound cites its component rows) are documented here but are never emitted in any output branch.

x-validator-contract.rule: Each element is a signal-table LITERAL id (S0..S16), NOT a Postgres step_runs row. M2: assert each id resolves to an existing Signal Table row; grammar enforces only the S-id string shape (pattern), not row existence.

R-cap / reclassify cap: the cap check STRICTLY PRECEDES classification. When the orchestrator-injected reclassify_count >= 3, the output MUST be result=ambiguous{disposition:escalate} with question == 'reclassify cap reached, manual intervention required' (no classification attempt — short-circuit before any signal-row evaluation, so a two-flow tie or any other classification outcome can NEVER be reached at cap). This cap precedence runs only AFTER Activity-0 INTERNAL shape/precondition asserts pass (a malformed input is error(INTERNAL) and never reaches the cap; see Activity-0 precedence). The exact-string equality assertion is SCOPED to reclassify_count>=3 ONLY; it does NOT fire for any other escalate. reclassify_count is an INPUT the grammar never sees, so this forced-escalation is a deterministic code check on (input, output), NOT a schema keyword.

Escalate-reason string contract for NON-cap escalates, which can occur ONLY when reclassify_count < 3 (when count>=3 the cap short-circuits before classification, so no non-cap escalate is reachable at cap — the two scopes are disjoint, never overlapping on the same input). PRODUCTION CONDITION (the deterministic decision path that reaches this branch, so it is not an unreachable promise): Activity-2 routes a multiple-rows-matched / no-superset case to disposition=escalate (instead of disposition=clarify) IFF no routing-blocking question can be formed — i.e. NO single verb/target/urgency/concern axis separates the candidate rows (an undecidable tie clarify cannot resolve); if such a separating axis exists the case routes to disposition=clarify instead. When disposition=escalate is reached at reclassify_count < 3 for that reason (or otherwise undecidable / upstream-deficient), question MUST be a non-empty one-line escalate reason naming the cause, and MUST NOT be the reserved cap string 'reclassify cap reached, manual intervention required' (that string is reserved for the reclassify_count>=3 cap path only). The reclassify_cap equality check above does not apply here.

prior_evidence cross-field precondition: prior_evidence is the reclassify-context input, so its presence IMPLIES an active reclassify state (reclassify_count >= 1). If prior_evidence is present while reclassify_count == 0 (or omitted, defaulting to 0), the upstream is stale/inconsistent and the output MUST be error(INTERNAL) -> orchestrator-level halt (the confidence low/medium and disagreement-table triggers would otherwise reference a non-existent reclassify state). reclassify_count and prior_evidence are INPUTS the grammar never sees, so this `prior_evidence present => reclassify_count >= 1` invariant is a deterministic Activity-0 precondition code check on (input, output), enforced in 0a BEFORE the cap check, NOT a schema keyword.

reclassify-active cross-field precondition (SYMMETRIC to prior_evidence_implies_reclassify_active): reclassify_count >= 1 claims an active reclassify, so it IMPLIES prior_evidence is present. If reclassify_count >= 1 AND reclassify_count < 3 while prior_evidence is absent, the upstream is inconsistent and the output MUST be error(INTERNAL) -> orchestrator-level halt (otherwise the confidence low(a)/low(b)/medium and disagreement-table branches would enter a reclassify state with no prior to read — silent garbage-in). SCOPE: bounded to reclassify_count < 3 because reclassify_count >= 3 short-circuits to the cap escalate (reclassify_cap_forces_ambiguous_escalate) BEFORE any classification and needs no prior_evidence. Together with prior_evidence_implies_reclassify_active this completes the invariant `prior_evidence present <=> reclassify_count >= 1` (for count < 3). reclassify_count and prior_evidence are INPUTS the grammar never sees; this is a deterministic Activity-0 precondition code check on (input, output), enforced in 0a BEFORE the cap check, NOT a schema keyword.

prior_evidence.evidence non-empty-shape precondition: when prior_evidence is present, its `evidence` must be an INFORMATIVE non-empty carrier — either a non-empty (non-whitespace-only) string OR a structured object {prior_matched_rows, summary} with a non-empty (non-whitespace-only) summary. A missing, empty-string, or whitespace-only evidence (and a structured evidence whose summary is empty/whitespace-only) is malformed and the output MUST be error(INTERNAL) -> orchestrator-level halt. Mere PRESENCE is insufficient: evidence is fed directly into Comprehend (Activity 1) input, so empty/garbage evidence would be silently trusted (P8 garbage-in defense). prior_evidence is an INPUT the grammar never sees; this Activity-0 shape assert is a deterministic (input, output) code check, NOT a schema keyword.

prior_evidence carrier-suppression: for result=none AND result=error, prior_evidence is IGNORED / not carried (disagreement-table 'prior ignored' rule: a none/error is not a classification result, so prior re-confirm/correct has no meaning). This contract asserts NO prior_evidence-derived field is emitted on the none or error branch. It is VACUOUS-BY-SHAPE by construction — the none branch carries only {result, reasoning} and the error branch only {result, reason} (unevaluatedProperties:false on both), so neither branch has any slot that could leak a prior_evidence-derived value; M2 records the rule so the 'prior ignored for none/GIBBERISH' assertion (mirroring prior_evidence_implies_reclassify_active) is enforced rather than left as unenforced garbage-in. prior_evidence is an INPUT the grammar never sees, so this is a deterministic (input, output) code check, NOT a schema keyword.

conversation_context cross-field precondition: the shared _defs Channel def declares 'conversation_context exists only for user_session', so conversation_context present IMPLIES channel == user_session. If conversation_context is present while channel != user_session (a2a or ci), the upstream is stale/malformed and the output MUST be error(INTERNAL) -> orchestrator-level halt (a malformed/stale upstream must not be silently trusted and fed into Comprehend — P8 garbage-in defense for exactly the invariant the shared schema declares). channel and conversation_context are INPUTS the grammar never sees, so this `conversation_context present => channel == user_session` invariant is a deterministic Activity-0 precondition code check on (input, output), enforced in 0a alongside the channel-enum and conversation_context-shape asserts, NOT a schema keyword.

clarifications has NO cross-field precondition — explicit lane decision (closes the asymmetry vs prior_evidence_implies_reclassify_active and conversation_context_implies_user_session_channel). Unlike prior_evidence (=> reclassify_count >= 1) and conversation_context (=> channel == user_session), clarifications carries NO analogous invariant: it may accompany ANY channel and ANY reclassify_count (including 0), because the clarify loop (accumulated Q&A answering a prior ambiguous{disposition:clarify}) is INDEPENDENT of reclassify state and does not imply reclassify_count >= 1. This M2 contract records the DELIBERATE absence of a cross-field constraint so the 'is stale clarifications rejected?' question is answered (NO — clarifications is trusted on shape alone, per the Activity-0 shape assert) rather than left as silent garbage-in ambiguity. clarifications is an INPUT the grammar never sees; this is a recorded no-op constraint making the (input,output) contract self-consistent, NOT a schema keyword.

The emitted flow_type must be consistent with the matched signal rows (the signal->flow mapping in the Signal Table). compound requires verb/target>=2 (>=2 distinct matched COMPONENT rows S1..S15, never the S16 meta-row — S16 maps documentation-wise to compound but is FORBIDDEN in matched_rows, exactly like S0; the grammar pattern ^S([1-9]|1[0-5])$ already excludes it); bugfix-p0 wins over bugfix on strict superset, and a strict-superset proceed cites ONLY the winning superset row (matched_rows == [S3], a single element — the subsumed S2 is re-derived by M2, never carried). Grammar fixes the enum value but cannot verify it matches the cited rows — M2 re-derives flow_type from matched_rows.

confidence=high requires either (a) FIRST classification (reclassify_count==0, prior_evidence absent) with exactly one matched signal row, OR (b) flow_type=compound, whose defining basis is >=2 non-superset matched rows (high regardless of reclassify — see compound_confidence_high_dominates_reclassify). NON-compound proceed carries EXACTLY ONE matched_rows element: a strict superset cites ONLY the winning (superset) row (e.g. [S3], never [S2,S3]) — the subsumed row is NOT emitted in matched_rows; M2 superset detection RE-DERIVES the subsumed row from the winner rather than expecting it in matched_rows, so it never sees 2 elements for a superset proceed and the multi-row=>ambiguous rule does not misfire on supersets. flow_type=compound is EXEMPT from the 'multiple non-superset rows => ambiguous' assertion: for non-compound proceed, if multiple non-superset rows matched, the correct output is the ambiguous branch, not proceed; for compound, >=2 non-superset rows are expected and yield confidence=high. M2 cross-checks confidence against the match cardinality, applying the multi-row=>ambiguous rule only to non-compound flow_types, and treats any matched_rows.length>1 on a non-compound proceed as a contract violation (only compound may emit multiple matched_rows).

compound-confidence dominance: flow_type=compound (>=2 distinct matched COMPONENT rows S1..S15) ALWAYS yields confidence=high, regardless of reclassify_count or whether the classification CORRECTS a different prior_flow_type. The compound rule (>=2 component rows => high) DOMINATES both the single-row reclassify-re-confirm rule (which would say medium) and the correcting-reclassify rule low(b) (which applies to SINGLE-row corrections only). A reclassify that newly classifies as compound (verb/target>=2 across a flow set different from prior_evidence.prior_flow_type) is high, NOT low(b) — resolving the otherwise-colliding confidence_consistent_with_match_cardinality (compound=>high) vs low(b) (correcting reclassify) contracts in favour of compound. reclassify_count and prior_evidence are INPUTS the grammar never sees; this is a deterministic (input, output) code check, NOT a schema keyword.

When result=proceed, classification_metadata.complexity_signal MUST be one of {high, medium, low} — NEVER 'none'. 'none' is the absence sentinel for non-proceed results (where classification_metadata does not exist) and is mutually exclusive with high/medium/low; it can never legitimately appear on a proceed. The shared ComplexitySignal enum keeps 'none' (other contexts need it), so grammar (M1) cannot exclude one member from the shared $ref — this proceed-only restriction is a sibling M2 code check.
